features.summary: Disney reported strong Q1 2022 results with adjusted EPS of $1.06 versus $0.32 prior year. Domestic Parks achieved record revenue and operating income despite lower attendance than 2019, driven by 40%+ higher per capita spending from premium services like Genie+ and improved guest mix. Disney+ added 11.8 million subscribers to reach 129.8 million globally, with growth from bundle inclusions, new market launches, and organic growth. The Media and Entertainment segment saw revenue growth but operating income declined due to higher programming costs, though advertising revenue hit records.
features.management_outlook: Management remains confident in achieving 230-260 million Disney+ subscribers by fiscal 2024, emphasizing content expansion including general entertainment integration and 340+ local international productions. They expect Disney+ to reach breakeven by fiscal 2024 with content spending approaching steady state by 2023. Parks demand pipeline remains strong with reservation systems enabling strategic attendance management. Programming costs are expected to increase $800M-$1B for Direct-to-Consumer and $500M for Linear Networks in Q2, with full-year content spending reaching $33 billion including sports rights.
features.risks: International parks face continued COVID volatility with Hong Kong temporarily closed due to case resurgence. Theatrical recovery remains prolonged, particularly for family animation and general entertainment films. Content sales licensing faces $200M+ adverse impact in Q2 due to strategic content retention for streaming services. Disney+ Hotstar growth depends partly on IPL rights renewal, though management believes they can achieve guidance without it. Production delays beyond COVID could impact content delivery timelines.
features.key_drivers: Parks performance driven by yield management through reservation systems, premium paid services adoption (over 50% during holidays), and pent-up domestic demand offsetting international visitor absence. Disney+ growth fueled by content slate expansion, bundle strategy success, and international market expansion with localized content. Advertising revenue strength across live sports and streaming platforms supporting overall segment performance. Technology investments in guest experience and content distribution platforms positioning for long-term growth in interactive entertainment and metaverse opportunities.
